title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox start 2026 season with 1-5 record, worst start since 2019, after Houston Astros sweep.
Boston's pitching pickups allowed 11 earned runs over 12 innings, with starter Garrett Crochet giving up five runs.
Red Sox defense committed six errors in six games after leading MLB with 116 errors last season.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
Diggs pleaded not guilty to charges stemming from a dispute with his personal chef over unpaid work.
The New England Patriots support Diggs, who denies the allegations, citing them as an employee-employer dispute.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Scott Quigley, a State Police sergeant, was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while driving under the influence in 2023.
The Middlesex DA's office was allegedly not informed by police about the fatal crash involving Quigley until months later.
Judge Thomas Drechsler's report criticized police reports for omitting key details, including Quigley's alleged 0.11 BAC at crash time.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, was sentenced to 15-20 years for the 2023 stabbing death of 21-year-old Jazreanna Sheppard.
The incident stemmed from an online feud and occurred at the Park Street MBTA station near Boston Common.
Partsch had a previous record, including a 2017 conviction for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Connecticut Sun's WNBA franchise relocates to Houston, rebranding as the Comets, reviving an historic team.
Kevin Durant acknowledges Connecticut's loss but highlights UConn women's basketball as a consolation for fans.
Former Celtics minority owner Steve Pagliuca's $325M bid to bring the Sun to Boston was blocked by WNBA.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Carlos Narvaez was scratched from the Red Sox lineup hours before playing against the Houston Astros on Wednesday.
Manager Alex Cora replaced Narvaez with Connor Wong, citing the decision as a needed change without elaborating further.
Narvaez confirmed he was not injured and expressed respect for the decision, keeping specifics private alongside Cora.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots plan to pick up Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option, keeping him through the 2027 season.
Gonzalez's base salary will rise from $2.2 million to $18.1 million with the option.
Coach Mike Vrabel expressed the team's interest in securing Gonzalez with a long-term contract extension.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm scored seven points in his last six games, aiding the Boston Bruins' recent four-game winning streak.
Relegated to a third-line role after a slump, Lindholm's resurgence boosts Bruins' playoff prospects under coach Marco Sturm.
The Lindholm-Reichel-Geekie line's scoring surge supports Boston's strong offense, as key players like Pastrnak continue to excel.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson of Everett runs the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson's participation honors trauma care improvements following the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings.
The Boston Marathon has personal significance for Davidson, reflecting on the city's resilient response to tragedy.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit largely due to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
City officials have delayed hiring and frozen some spending to manage the deficit, which is 1% of the $4.8 billion budget.
Boston Public Schools also faces a separate $53 million deficit, with potential cuts of 400 positions across the district.
